summary: American minimalist/post-minimalist composer (b. 1947), full name John Coolidge Adams. Best known for the operas Nixon in China (1987) and The Death of Klinghoffer (1991), and the orchestral works Harmonielehre, Short Ride in a Fast Machine, and On the Transmigration of Souls (a 9/11 memorial that won the 2002 Pulitzer Prize). His frequent collaborator is director Peter Sellars. Not to be confused with John Luther Adams (Alaskan composer of Become Ocean).

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: John Coolidge Adams (b. 1947) is an American minimalist and post-minimalist composer. He frequently collaborates with theater director Peter Sellars, who has directed and written libretti for many of his operas. His website is earbox.com. He should not be confused with John Luther Adams (an Alaskan composer best known for Become Ocean). Adams described his pieces as coming in twos — 'serious' and 'trickster.' He depicted his dreams of an oil tanker taking off from water and two limousines transforming into pianos in Harmonielehre and Grand Pianola Music. He has been associated with the San Francisco Symphony and California arts institutions. Wikipedia

works.name: Nixon in China
works.description: Three-act opera premiered in 1987, with libretto by Alice Goodman and direction by Peter Sellars. Depicts Nixon's 1972 visit to Beijing to meet Chairman Mao. Opens with the chorus 'The people are the heroes now' as the presidential plane 'Spirit of '76' lands in Beijing airfield. Includes an aria called 'News Has a Kind of Mystery' and a scene where Nixon and Mao's wife attend a performance of The Red Detachment of Women. The work was preceded by the orchestral piece The Chairman Dances. Pat Nixon sings 'This is prophetic' in the Summer Palace. Soprano Dawn Upshaw's recording includes her singing 'This Is Prophetic.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Depicts Nixon's 1972 visit to China; opens with the chorus 'The people are the heroes now' as the Spirit of '76 lands; Nixon attends The Red Detachment of Women
examples: An opera by this composer opens with the chorus 'The people are the heroes now' in an airfield before the 'Spirit of '76' lands, and includes a part in which the title character attends a performance of The Red Detachment of Women.
His best-known direction of an opera involved putting the presidential airplane 'Spirit of '76' and a giant bomb on the stage.

works.name: The Death of Klinghoffer
works.description: Opera (1991) about the 1985 hijacking of the cruise ship Achille Lauro and the murder of wheelchair-bound American Leon Klinghoffer. The protagonist sings the 'Aria of the Falling Body' as he is thrown overboard. Libretto by Alice Goodman; directed by Peter Sellars. Act II opens with the chorus 'Hagar and the Angel.' A character is shot by the Palestinian hijacker Molqi. Wikipedia

clues.clue: About the Achille Lauro hijacking; the protagonist sings the 'Aria of the Falling Body' as he is thrown overboard; Act II includes 'Hagar and the Angel'; a character is shot by Molqi
examples: this composer of another opera in which the protagonist sings the 'Aria of the Falling Body' as he is thrown overboard the Achille Lauro
Act II of that opera by this composer opens with the chorus 'Hagar and the Angel' and ends when a character is shot by Molqi.

works.name: Doctor Atomic
works.description: Opera (2005) about J. Robert Oppenheimer and the Manhattan Project/Trinity test. Oppenheimer sings 'Batter my heart, three-person'd God' (a John Donne sonnet). The opera uses a traditional Tewa song and the Bhagavad Gita. Libretto by Peter Sellars. Wikipedia

examples: J. Robert Oppenheimer sings, 'Batter my heart, three person'd God' in this minimalist composer's opera Doctor Atomic.
this composer is known for an opera that uses part of a traditional Tewa song and the Bhagavad Gita

works.name: On the Transmigration of Souls
works.description: A 'memory space' for orchestra, children's choir, and a tape that plays city sounds (traffic, footsteps, a siren) and reads the names of those who died in the September 11 attacks, from the New York Times's 'Portraits of Grief.' A boy's voice repeats the word 'missing' in the rhythm of a heartbeat. Won the 2003 Pulitzer Prize for Music. Wikipedia

clues.clue: 9/11 memorial work: a tape reads names of the dead from the New York Times's 'Portraits of Grief'; city sounds (traffic, footsteps, siren) play; a boy's voice says 'missing' in a heartbeat rhythm
examples: This composer called for tape recordings of city traffic, footsteps, and a siren at the start of a piece played against the backdrop of voices reciting of a long list of names.
A boy's voice repeats the word 'missing' in the rhythm of a heartbeat at the start of a 'memory space' by this composer that sets text from the New York Times's 'Portraits of Grief'

works.name: Short Ride in a Fast Machine
works.description: Fanfare for orchestra (1986), composed for the Pittsburgh Symphony's Great Woods Festival. Inspired by the feeling of riding in a terrific sports car and then wishing you hadn't. Opens with a woodblock ostinato that creates a 'rhythmic tunnel.' Contains an 'almost sadistic' ostinato. Wikipedia

clues.clue: A 'fanfare for orchestra' opening with a woodblock ostinato; inspired by the feeling of riding in a terrific sports car then wishing you hadn't; contains an 'almost sadistic' ostinato
examples: name this composer of a 'fanfare for orchestra' titled Short Ride in a Fast Machine
This composer included an ostinato that he described as 'almost sadistic' in a piece inspired by the feeling 'when someone asks you to ride in a terrific sports car, and then you wish you hadn't.'

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Harmonielehre (1985): large orchestral work; the composer depicted his dreams of an oil tanker taking off from water and two limousines transforming into pianos. Shaker Loops (for strings): 'Hymning Slews' and 'Loops and Verses' are inner movements; reminds Adams of the energetic dancing of the Shakers. Phrygian Gates: piano piece beginning in the Lydian mode of A, oscillating between two modes while modulating around the circle of fifths. El Niño: nativity oratorio; Dawn Upshaw sang the soprano part at the premiere. Girls of the Golden West (2017): opera inspired by Puccini's La fanciulla del West; Puccini-inspired, set during the California Gold Rush; libretto by Peter Sellars. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Harmonielehre — large orchestral work; composer depicted dreams of an oil tanker lifting off and two limousines turning into pianos
examples: a composer depicted his dreams of an oil tanker taking off from a body of water and two limousines transforming into pianos in his two pieces Harmonielehre and Grand Pianola Music.

clues.clue: Shaker Loops — for strings; inner movements 'Hymning Slews' and 'Loops and Verses'; reminds Adams of Shakers' energetic dancing
examples: 'Hymning Slews' and 'Loops and Verses' are the inner movements of a piece by this composer said to remind him of the energetic dancing of the title sect.

clues.clue: Phrygian Gates — piano work beginning in the Lydian mode of A, oscillating between two modes while modulating around the circle of fifths
examples: A piano piece by this composer begins in the Lydian mode of A and oscillates between two modes as it repeatedly modulates around the circle of fifths.

clues.clue: El Niño — nativity oratorio; Dawn Upshaw sang soprano at the premiere
examples: Upshaw sang the soprano part at the premiere of this composer's 'nativity oratorio' El Nino.

clues.clue: Girls of the Golden West (2017) — libretto by Peter Sellars; inspired by Puccini's La fanciulla del West; set during the California Gold Rush
examples: A Puccini-inspired opera by this composer is based on letters written during the California Gold Rush.
Sellars wrote another libretto for Adams's most recent opera, titled for Girls of this specific two-word region [Golden West].

comprehensive_summary:
John Coolidge Adams (b. 1947) is an American minimalist and post-minimalist composer, best known for his operas Nixon in China and The Death of Klinghoffer, his orchestral fanfare Short Ride in a Fast Machine, and his 9/11 memorial On the Transmigration of Souls. His website is earbox.com, and he is frequently associated with California — he was affiliated with the San Francisco Conservatory and the San Francisco Symphony.

His operas are closely tied to theater director Peter Sellars, who has directed and often written libretti for them. Nixon in China (1987), with libretto by Alice Goodman, depicts Richard Nixon's 1972 visit to Beijing. It opens with the chorus 'The people are the heroes now' as Air Force One (the 'Spirit of '76') lands in a Beijing airfield. Nixon and Mao's wife attend a performance of The Red Detachment of Women. Pat Nixon's aria 'This is prophetic' is sung in the Summer Palace. The opera was preceded by the orchestral piece The Chairman Dances.

The Death of Klinghoffer (1991), also with libretto by Alice Goodman and direction by Peter Sellars, dramatizes the 1985 hijacking of the cruise ship Achille Lauro. Leon Klinghoffer sings the 'Aria of the Falling Body' as he is thrown overboard. Act II opens with the chorus 'Hagar and the Angel' and ends when a character is shot by the hijacker Molqi.

Doctor Atomic (2005), with a libretto by Sellars, dramatizes J. Robert Oppenheimer and the Trinity nuclear test. Oppenheimer sings a setting of John Donne's sonnet 'Batter my heart, three-person'd God'; the opera also uses a traditional Tewa song and the Bhagavad Gita.

On the Transmigration of Souls (2002) is a 'memory space' for orchestra, children's choir, and a tape playing city sounds (traffic, footsteps, a siren) and reading the names of those who died in the September 11 attacks, drawn from the New York Times's 'Portraits of Grief.' A boy's voice repeats 'missing' in a heartbeat rhythm. It won the 2003 Pulitzer Prize.

Short Ride in a Fast Machine (1986) is a fanfare for orchestra composed for the Pittsburgh Symphony's Great Woods Festival. Inspired by the feeling of riding in a fast sports car and then regretting it, it features a woodblock ostinato that creates a 'rhythmic tunnel.' Other notable works: Harmonielehre (in which Adams depicted dreaming of an oil tanker lifting off from water and two limousines turning into pianos); Shaker Loops for strings (inner movements 'Hymning Slews' and 'Loops and Verses'); Phrygian Gates (piano; begins in the Lydian mode of A); the nativity oratorio El Niño (Dawn Upshaw sang the premiere); and the 2017 opera Girls of the Golden West (inspired by Puccini's La fanciulla del West).

Adams cited Conlon Nancarrow's player piano works as inspiration for Century Rolls (movements 'Manny's Gym' and 'Hail, Bop') and Grand Pianola Music. He should not be confused with John Luther Adams (an Alaskan composer whose piece Become Ocean won the 2014 Pulitzer Prize for Music and a Grammy).

cards.front: Composer: American minimalist; operas include Nixon in China, The Death of Klinghoffer, and Doctor Atomic; On the Transmigration of Souls won the 2003 Pulitzer
Composer: frequent collaborator with theater director Peter Sellars, who directed and wrote libretti for Nixon in China, The Death of Klinghoffer, Doctor Atomic, and Girls of the Golden West
Opera: depicts Nixon's 1972 visit to China; opens with the Spirit of '76 landing; Nixon attends The Red Detachment of Women; preceded by The Chairman Dances
Opera: libretto by Alice Goodman; Pat Nixon sings 'This is prophetic' in the Summer Palace; aria 'News Has a Kind of Mystery' is sung after a jet lands in Beijing
Opera: about the Achille Lauro hijacking; the protagonist sings 'Aria of the Falling Body' as he is thrown overboard; a character is shot by the hijacker Molqi
Opera: about J. Robert Oppenheimer; Oppenheimer sings 'Batter my heart, three-person'd God'; uses a Tewa song and the Bhagavad Gita; libretto by Peter Sellars
Oratorio: 9/11 memorial; tape reads names of the dead and plays city sounds; a boy's voice repeats 'missing' in a heartbeat rhythm; won the 2003 Pulitzer Prize
Overture: 'fanfare for orchestra' for Pittsburgh Symphony (1986); opens with a woodblock ostinato; inspired by the feeling of riding in a fast sports car then regretting it
